name: write
context: Insert, update, replace, delete, and bulk-write MongoDB documents with acknowledged results
info: This sub-skill focuses on write operations and their result objects: creating documents with generated or supplied `_id` values, modifying matching documents with update operators, replacing a full document, deleting selectively, and batching heterogeneous writes with `bulkWrite()`. Agents use it for app mutations, cleanup jobs, backfills, and repair scripts where the exact matched, modified, inserted, or deleted counts matter.

sections.title: Insert and `_id` handling
sections.body: Every MongoDB document needs a unique `_id`. The Node.js driver generates one automatically when you omit it, which is the safest default. Provide your own `_id` only when you control uniqueness and need stable identifiers across systems.

sections.title: Updates and upserts
sections.body: Use operator updates such as `$set`, `$unset`, `$inc`, and `$currentDate` when changing parts of a document. Use `upsert: true` when the workflow should create the document if it does not already exist. Inspect `matchedCount`, `modifiedCount`, and `upsertedId` so the caller knows what happened.

sections.title: Bulk write semantics
sections.body: Use `bulkWrite()` to batch mixed operations and reduce round trips. `ordered: true` stops at the first failure, which is safer for tightly coupled changes. `ordered: false` allows the server to continue past failures, which is useful for best-effort cleanup or large migrations that should maximize throughput.

links.context: Parent CRUD skill for mixed read/write workflows
Writes must conform to collection validators when they exist
Escalate to a transaction when related writes must commit together

related.note: Validators can reject inserts and updates that violate collection rules
Use transactions when the write spans multiple documents or collections that must commit together

scripts.name: Write documents
scripts.description: Run a focused MongoDB write operation or bulkWrite batch using Extended JSON input

WRITE_INPUT_JSON.description: Extended JSON payload. If omitted, the script reads JSON from stdin.

author: dojo-community
